methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $114M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Speedata, a Series B semiconductor company founded by Jonathan Friedmann and Rami Dahan, has raised $114M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, Substrate, DEEPX, and one other) plus 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, using the $114M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yields $217M. Peer-median valuation across the 6-company comp set produces $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.6x across semiconductors) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures. The three valuation results—$217M, $1.0B, and an inapplicable public method—triangulate to a median of $608M with a range from $184M (low) to $1.1B (high).

The wide spread reflects meaningful uncertainty: peer valuations span an order of magnitude, driven partly by stage and market timing variance. The $608M median sits substantially above the stage-progression floor but well below the peer median, suggesting a conservative positioning relative to recent private market comps. Key unknowns include capital deployment efficiency, unit economics, and revenue trajectory—data not available in this analysis.
